title: Expand session detail cards into full inspection panes
summary: Extended the session cards into denser detail panes so goals, changes, rationale, files, and tool usage can be inspected without leaving the frontend dashboard.
goal: Make the session list a real inspection tool where a reviewer can understand why a frontend session happened, not just when it updated.
changesMade: Added expandable detail sections for goal, changes, features, decisions, blockers, and tools
Tightened card spacing so large sessions remain scan-friendly in TSX
Improved file and tool badges for high-volume frontend sessions
Aligned session detail density with the activity rail and shell patterns

features.name: full session inspection pane
features.why: Operators need the stored context in the UI, not only the raw JSON files in .handoffs.

features.name: dense badge rows for files and tools
features.why: Large frontend sessions touch many TSX and JSX files, so the card needs compact metadata presentation.

features.name: inspection-friendly section order
features.why: Reviewers usually look for summary, goal, rationale, and touched files in that order.

decisions.decision: Use inline expansion rather than modal drill-down for session details
decisions.why: Inspecting many sessions in sequence is faster if the operator can stay in one scroll context.

decisions.decision: Lead with recorded rationale before low-level file counts
decisions.why: The whole point of the handoff repo is preserving intent; the UI should reinforce that priority.

openQuestions: Do we want keyboard navigation between expanded session cards once the list is long?
resumePrompt: Continue the session inspection pane by tightening long-form content layout in dashboard/src/components/dashboard.tsx.
